codeReview.summary: Reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill file, and commit 6b898df768bdff3e4c70de76faa16c3004bcecfe. The feature adds approval prompting, y/n handling, idle-aware /stop behavior, and menu routing, but it still misses the validation contract's cancelled-approval requirement because pending approval state is only cleared by background polling; I also found a new fire-and-forget approval-prompt path that can raise an unhandled rejection during attach.

issues.description: `handlePrompt()` treats any non-command text as an approval reply whenever `pendingApprovals` still contains an entry, but that map is only cleared later by the forwarding poller when it eventually notices `turn/completed` at lines 650-653. Because the default poll interval is 1s, a user prompt sent immediately after Codex cancels the approval can still be rejected as a malformed y/n reply instead of being forwarded, so the implementation does not reliably satisfy the feature/validation requirement that cancelled approvals clear pending state (`features.json:619-630`, `validation-contract.md:186-187`).

When a user attaches to a session that is already in `waitingApproval`, `startForwarding()` fire-and-forgets `presentApprovalRequest()` with `void`. That callee awaits `this.reply()` at lines 714-720, so any Feishu send failure in this new attach-time approval path bypasses the surrounding request error handling and becomes an unhandled rejection instead of a normal bot error response.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The architecture docs still describe the bot as holding only the in-memory user→session attach mapping, but this feature adds additional long-lived in-memory caches (`lastSeenChats` and `pendingApprovals`). If those caches are now part of the intended design, the statelessness invariant should be updated so future workers do not assume attach state is the bot's only persisted runtime state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:88-90 and 157-159 say the bot is stateless except for attach mapping; bot/src/bot-service.ts:85-89 now adds `lastSeenChats` and `pendingApprovals`, and bot/src/bot-service.ts:121, 164-166, 502, 651-653 actively relies on them for menu actions and approval handling.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared docs do not record that Feishu `application.bot.menu_v6` events lack a `chat_id`, so menu replies depend on cached chat context from earlier text traffic or an active attachment. That constraint drove the new `lastSeenChats` behavior and is worth documenting for future bot work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: node_modules/@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk/types/index.d.ts:285462-285480 defines `application.bot.menu_v6` with operator IDs and `event_key` but no chat identifier; bot/src/feishu.ts:187-205 therefore returns only `{userId,eventKey}`, and bot/src/bot-service.ts:87, 121, 164-166 now caches/reuses chat IDs to answer menu actions.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit 6b898df768bdff3e4c70de76faa16c3004bcecfe. Approval prompting, /stop, and menu routing are mostly in place, but cancelled approvals are only cleared on the next polling pass, so immediate post-cancel prompts can still be mishandled; I also noted a smaller unhandled-rejection risk in the attach-time approval prompt path.
